architecture_summary: RT-1 is a Transformer-based robot policy that takes a history of images and a natural-language instruction, encodes them with a FiLM-conditioned EfficientNet-B3 and TokenLearner, and outputs discretized robot actions with a decoder-only Transformer for real-time control.

novel_contributions: We present a model class, dubbed Robotics Transformer, that exhibits promising scalable model properties.

We propose a novel architecture that we call RT-1 (Robotics Transformer 1), which encodes high-dimensional inputs and outputs into compact token representations to enable efficient inference at runtime.
We show that RT-1 can perform over 700 training instructions at 97% success rate and generalize to new tasks, distractors, and backgrounds better than prior techniques.
We show that RT-1 can incorporate data from simulation or even other robot types, retaining performance on the original tasks and improving generalization to new scenarios.

key_claims.claim: RT-1 can perform over 700 training instructions at 97% success rate and generalize to new tasks, distractors, and backgrounds better than the next best baseline.
RT-1 takes images and natural language instructions and outputs discretized base and arm actions at 3 Hz, despite its size (35M parameters).
The model uses a FiLM-conditioned EfficientNet-B3, TokenLearner, and a decoder-only Transformer to achieve efficient real-time control.
RT-1 is able to perform 97% of seen tasks, 76% of unseen tasks, 83% of distractor tasks, and 59% of background tasks.
key_claims.evidence_section: 6.2 CAN RT-1 LEARN TO PERFORM A LARGE NUMBER OF INSTRUCTIONS, AND TO GENERALIZE TO NEW TASKS, OBJECTS AND ENVIRONMENTS?

key_claims.claim: RT-1 can execute long-horizon SayCan tasks with 67% execution success rate in both Kitchen1 and Kitchen2.
key_claims.evidence_section: 6.4 HOW DO VARIOUS METHODS GENERALIZE LONG-HORIZON ROBOTIC SCENARIOS?

key_claims.claim: Adding simulation data does not hurt real-object performance and improves performance on simulation-only objects from 23% to 87% and unseen instructions from 7% to 33%.
key_claims.evidence_section: 6.3 CAN WE PUSH THE RESULTING MODEL FURTHER BY INCORPORATING HETEROGENEOUS DATA SOURCES SUCH AS SIMULATION OR DATA FROM DIFFERENT ROBOTS?

key_claims.claim: Mixing Kuka bin-picking data with EDR data minimally impacts classroom performance and improves bin-picking eval from 22% to 39%.
key_claims.evidence_section: 6.3 CAN WE PUSH THE RESULTING MODEL FURTHER BY INCORPORATING HETEROGENEOUS DATA SOURCES SUCH AS SIMULATION OR DATA FROM DIFFERENT ROBOTS?

key_claims.claim: Data diversity has a higher impact on performance and generalization than data quantity.
key_claims.evidence_section: 6.5 HOW DO GENERALIZATION METRICS CHANGE WITH VARYING AMOUNTS OF DATA QUANTITY AND DATA DIVERSITY?
